_meta.description: Deterministic tiebreak rules when a document matches detection rules in two or more domains simultaneously. The winner domain takes priority for doc-type assignment.

rules.reason: Billing is the specialized domain for utility bills; everyday is a catch-all. Prefer specificity.
Medical documents require domain-specific safety rules and disclaimers. Medical takes precedence over generic billing.
Medical content (lab reports, clinical notes) takes precedence. Insurance EOBs stay in insurance domain.
Employment agreements are legal instruments first. HR/payroll handles pay stubs and operational docs.
When a financial statement matches both, prefer finance for investor-facing docs. Accounting handles internal ledgers and GL.
Mortgage statements and property tax bills are housing-specific. Generic billing is for utilities.
Tax returns and notices are tax-specific. Accounting handles internal tax reconciliation worksheets.
Identity documents (passport, driver license) require specialized extraction. Everyday is a catch-all.
Insurance premium invoices are insurance-specific. Generic billing handles utility invoices.
Consumer bank statements belong to banking. Finance handles investment-grade financial reports.
Bank statements are banking-specific. Everyday is a catch-all.
Transcripts and diplomas are education-specific. Everyday is a catch-all.
Boarding passes and itineraries are travel-specific. Everyday is a catch-all.
Pay stubs and offer letters are HR-specific. Everyday is a catch-all.
Tax returns are tax-specific. Everyday is a catch-all.
Medical documents require safety boundaries. Must never be treated as everyday.
Legal contracts require clause-level extraction. Must never be treated as everyday.
Financial statements require numeric integrity rules. Must never be treated as everyday.
